Savings_DesiredAgeToBeMillionaire.fieldLabel: Desired age to be a millionaire

labels.title: What will it take to become a millionaire?
labels.answer: You'll be a millionaire in %years% %yearlabel% at age %age%
labels.answer2: Sorry, it appears that you will not become a millionaire in your lifetime.
labels.answerText1:
You could still be a millionaire at age %age% if you:

  • Decrease the amount you invest now to %investNow%, or
  • Decrease your monthly investment to %monthlyInvest%, or
  • Earn a rate of return of %returnRate%%
When adjusted for inflation, $1 million in %years% %yearlabel% would be equivalent to %totAmount% today.
labels.answerText2:
To be a millionaire at age %age%, you'll need to:

  • Increase the amount you invest now to %investNow%, or
  • Increase your monthly investment to %monthlyInvest%, or
  • Earn a rate of return of %returnRate%%
When adjusted for inflation, $1 million in %years% %yearlabel% would be equivalent to %totAmount% today.
labels.answerText3:
You could still be a millionaire at age %age% if you:

  • Decrease the amount you invest now to %investNow%, or
  • Decrease your monthly investment to %monthlyInvest%
When adjusted for inflation, $1 million in %years% %yearlabel% would be equivalent to %totAmount% today.
labels.answerText4: When adjusted for inflation, $1 million in %years% %yearlabel% would be equivalent to %totAmount% today.

taxableaccount.title: Taxable account
taxableaccount.definition: An account that does not receive the tax breaks that either a tax-exempt account or tax-deferred account are eligible to receive.

inflationrate.title: Inflation rate
inflationrate.definition: A general increase in prices that you pay for goods and services, stated as a yearly rate. A common measure of inflation in the U.S. is the Consumer Price Index (CPI).

futurevalue.title: Future value
futurevalue.definition: The amount to which your investment grows in the future. For example, the future value of $100 invested monthly at 8% is $1,245 after 12 months.

taxrates.title: Tax rates
taxrates.definition: The percentage of your taxable income that is owed to the state and federal governments. The tax rate increases as the taxable base amount increases.

discount.title: Discount
discount.definition: A price that is less than the regular sale price. In bond investing, a discount is a bond price that is less than the par value of the bond.

savingsinterestrate.title: Savings interest rate
savingsinterestrate.definition: The yearly interest rate you earn on your savings.

taxadvantagedaccount.title: Tax-advantaged account
taxadvantagedaccount.definition: An investment account with tax-deferred or tax-exempt features that are used to save for retirement, college, and other educational expenses.

lumpsuminvestments.title: Lump-sum investments
lumpsuminvestments.definition: An investment that you make to open an investment or savings account.

inflation.title: Inflation
inflation.definition: A general increase in prices that you pay for goods and services, stated as a yearly rate.

help.textLabel: This tool calculates how long it will take you to save $1 million based on the savings information that you provide. The future value of $1 million is discounted by the inflation rate.

The calculator assumes you save with a taxable account. If you want to simulate saving with a tax-advantaged account, enter a zero in the fields for federal and state income tax rates. Tax-advantaged accounts may have restrictions on taking early penalty-free distributions. You may wish to consult a financial professional.

chartTitles.title: Bar chart comparing the age that you are estimated to become a millionaire with your desired age to become a millionaire.

tableTitles.title: Age that you are estimated to become a millionaire with your desired age to become a millionaire
